signals.headline: YES** on "Iran ceasefire active" prediction markets (from April 8 agreement calming oil prices/markets temporarily), but
signals.raw: YES** on "Iran ceasefire active" prediction markets (from April 8 agreement calming oil prices/markets temporarily), but **NO** on permanent peace or major updates today—talks continue without resolution, with a second round possibly imminent per latest reports.[3][5][6][7]
matched_markets: Will the US x Iran ceasefire be extended by April 21, 2026?
US x Iran permanent peace deal by April 22, 2026?
US x Iran diplomatic meeting by April 18, 2026?

signals.headline: MED confidence** (solid on April 8 event from multiple sources; low on "RIGHT NOW" shifts due to no April 15 specifics,
signals.raw: MED confidence** (solid on April 8 event from multiple sources; low on "RIGHT NOW" shifts due to no April 15 specifics, only UN pleas and looming talks).[1][2][3][4][5][7]

signals.headline: South Korea's **headline inflation** eased to 2
signals.raw: South Korea's **headline inflation** eased to 2.0% YoY in January 2026 (from 2.3% in December), with **core inflation** steady at 2.0%, but the Bank of Korea plans to hold its policy rate at 2.5% with no imminent cuts.[1][6]
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Fed reportedly **froze rates** (maintaining 1
signals.raw: Fed reportedly **froze rates** (maintaining 1.25% gap with South Korea) amid Iran-related inflation fears and oil surges, dated March 19, 2026.[4]
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Trump administration's **Fed Chair nomination** (possibly Kevin Warsh) triggered a >5% **KOSPI drop** in early February
signals.raw: Trump administration's **Fed Chair nomination** (possibly Kevin Warsh) triggered a >5% **KOSPI drop** in early February 2026, reflecting market uncertainty.[2][5]
matched_markets: Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed Chair by May 15?

signals.headline: Global **core CPI forecast** for 2026 holds at 2
signals.raw: Global **core CPI forecast** for 2026 holds at 2.8%, with U.S. at 3.2%; South Korea near 2% supports BoK pause.[6]

signals.headline: Annual South Korea CPI for 2024 was 2
signals.raw:
Annual South Korea CPI for 2024 was 2.32% (no 2025/2026 annual data).[3]

No sources indicate announcements today or in April 2026; prior events (e.g., rate freeze, nomination) are 1-3 months old and unlikely to freshly impact prediction markets.[1][2][4][5][6]
